role_category: Applied ML / Information Retrieval / Search & Recommendation
core_problem: The current candidate matching/search system relies on brittle, low-leverage BM25 and rule-based scoring.
The company needs to quickly design, deploy, and scale a high-leverage v2 hybrid search and ranking engine that directly impacts user engagement.
Lack of evaluation infrastructure (A/B testing, offline benchmarks, feedback loops) makes it impossible to systematically improve search relevance without flying blind.
must_have_skills: Production experience with embedding-based retrieval models (sentence-transformers, BGE, E5, etc.)
Production management of vector databases or hybrid search index lifecycle (index refresh, handling drift, quality regression)
Rigorous knowledge of ranking evaluation metrics (NDCG, MRR, MAP, offline-to-online correlation)
nice_to_have_skills: Learning-to-Rank (LTR) model implementation (XGBoost, LambdaMART, neural ranking)
Experience with distributed systems and large-scale model inference optimization
Open-source contributions to AI/ML or search projects
required_systems_experience: Vector Databases (Pinecone, Weaviate, Qdrant, Milvus, FAISS)
Offline evaluation frameworks and online A/B testing infrastructure
behavioral_requirements: Pragmatic, 'shipper' mindset over academic or theoretical purity
High autonomy and comfortable with high ambiguity (Series A founding environment)
Strong async communication skills with a heavy emphasis on written documentation
Comfortable with open, direct intellectual debate and rapid decision-making cycles
hidden_requirements: Ability to work completely independently without an army of platform or infra engineers to deploy your models
Long-term retention alignment: looking for high-ownership individuals who want equity upside and plan to stay 3+ years, not title-chasers
An intuitive grasp of recruitment or HR-tech search challenges (e.g., matching unstructured resume data with unstructured job descriptions)
negative_signals: Frequent job hopping (switching companies every 1-1.5 years to climb titles)
Over-reliance on high-level orchestration frameworks like LangChain/LlamaIndex for basic tasks
Lack of public-facing code, open-source work, or papers if coming from closed-source backgrounds
Reluctance to write production-ready code (e.g., claiming to be an 'architect' or 'tech lead' who no longer codes)
disqualifiers: Career spent entirely in pure research/academic environments with zero production deployments
No pre-LLM era ML experience, or AI experience limited strictly to calling wrapper APIs (e.g., OpenAI API) for <12 months
No production code written in the past 18 months
Entire career spent at IT consulting/outsourcing firms (TCS, Infosys, Wipro, Accenture, Cognizant, etc.) without product-company experience
Primary expertise is purely in Computer Vision, Audio, or Robotics with no NLP/Search background

preferred_candidate_archetypes.name: The Product-Minded ML Shipper
preferred_candidate_archetypes.description: An engineer currently working at a fast-growing B2B/B2C marketplace or SaaS startup who has successfully built and deployed a search, match, or recommendation feed from scratch, prioritizing metrics and execution speed over academic perfection.

bad_candidate_archetypes.name: The Framework Wrapper Hype-Rider
bad_candidate_archetypes.description: An engineer whose resume is packed with trendy AI keywords, LangChain tutorials, and Toy GPT wrappers, but lacks foundational software engineering, mathematical intuition for retrieval, or classic IR evaluation metrics.

bad_candidate_archetypes.name: The Pure Research Academic
bad_candidate_archetypes.description: A researcher with a deep publication record in top AI conferences who is used to working on static datasets with infinite training time, but struggles to write clean production Python or optimize latency.

bad_candidate_archetypes.name: The Enterprise Consulting Engineer
bad_candidate_archetypes.description: An engineer with a career built inside giant IT services systems who expects highly structured task tickets, clear requirements, and deep organizational hierarchy, rather than chaotic, fast-paced startup execution.

must_have_evidence: A clear history of owning a production-scale ranking, search, or recommendation system at a product-first company
Demonstrated ability to write and ship clean, complex Python backend/ML code within the last year
Verifiable understanding of how they measured the business/relevance impact of their models (e.g., A/B test results, NDCG improvements)
location_requirements: Pune or Noida preferred (hybrid cadence), open to relocation from Tier-1 Indian cities (Delhi NCR, Bangalore, Hyderabad, Mumbai). No visa sponsorship.
what_the_jd_really_means: 'We'd rather you tilt slightly toward shipper than toward researcher' -> Do not waste time building an overly complex model when a simple, hybrid heuristics-and-embeddings approach can be shipped in 5 days to solve the user's immediate pain.
'We are growing the team from 4 to 12' -> You will need to wear many hats: writing code, designing systems, and mentoring junior hires simultaneously.
'We work async-first and write a lot' -> If you cannot clearly articulate your architectural decisions in written design docs, you will not survive our remote/async culture.

scoring_dimensions.name: Production Ranking & Retrieval Experience
scoring_dimensions.reason: The absolute core of the role is owning the matching/ranking architecture. Theoretical knowledge is useless; they must have managed production retrieval systems, index lifecycles, and embedding drift.

scoring_dimensions.name: Production-Grade Coding & Architecture (Python)
scoring_dimensions.reason: This is a founding team role that writes code daily. We disqualify architects who don't code and researchers who write dirty notebooks. Code quality and system architecture are vital.

scoring_dimensions.name: Evaluation Rigor & Metrics
scoring_dimensions.reason: If the candidate cannot scientifically evaluate if their model is actually better using NDCG, MRR, or online A/B testing, they will keep shipping blind changes.

scoring_dimensions.name: Product Startup Shipper Mindset
scoring_dimensions.reason: Prefers shipping pragmatic, working rankers quickly rather than spending 6 months optimizing a marginal accuracy increase. Disqualifies slow-moving corporate or purely academic profiles.

ideal_candidate_summary: A highly pragmatic Senior Applied ML / Search Engineer with 5 to 9 years of experience, currently working at a fast-paced product company or marketplace startup. They have hands-on experience building, scaling, and validating hybrid search and recommendation algorithms from scratch using vector databases and ranking metrics (NDCG/MRR). They are active IC coders in Python, communicate exceptionally well in writing, bias strongly toward execution speed over research perfection, and are located in or ready to relocate to Pune/Noida.
